content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based UI for the tc24r compiler using Rust/Yew/WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project, embed tc24r as WASM. Phase 2: editor component with syntax highlighting. Phase 3: compile-and-run workflow in browser.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-research-and-scaffold [production]: Study the tc24r compiler at ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r (read README, CLAUD...
  [x] 002-embed-tc24r [legacy]: Compile tc24r core to WASM, integrate into Yew app
  [x] 003-editor [legacy]: Add code editor component with syntax highlighting
  [ ] 004-compile-run [legacy]: Wire compile and run workflow in browser <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 005-output-display [legacy]: Show assembler output and emulator execution results

=== CURRENT STEP: 004-compile-run [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Wire compile and run workflow in browser

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

=== CURRENT STEP: 004-compile-run [legacy] ===

Description: Wire compile and run workflow in browser

=== WHEN DONE ===

Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.text:
The compile-and-run workflow is already fully wired in previous sessions:
- `compiler.rs`: Complete 6-stage pipeline (preprocess → lex → parse → codegen → assemble → execute)
- `main.rs`: 3-panel UI with "Compile & Run" button connected to the pipeline

Let me verify it works end-to-end.

The compile-and-run workflow works perfectly:

- **C Source**: Syntax-highlighted editor with the default "Hello + LED" program
- **Generated Assembly**: Full COR24 assembly output with function prologues/epilogues, UART writes, and LED control
- **Output**: `Hi!` printed via UART, program halted normally after 83 instructions, r0=0x2a (42 from `return 42`)
